title: Why Self-Care Is Mandatory for Moms: A Doctor's Guide to Holistic Motherhood
guestBio: Dr. Laura Brayton is a chiropractor, pediatric specialist, and functional medicine practitioner with over 20 years of experience. She hosts the Well-Adjusted Mama podcast and specializes in empowering women through conscious conception, healthy pregnancy, and joyful motherhood from her practice in Hoboken, NJ.
excerpt: Dr. Laura Brayton reveals why self-care isn't selfish—it's mandatory for every mother. Discover how chiropractic care transforms pregnancy outcomes, why preconception detox matters, and how to regulate your nervous system for better leadership.
metaDescription: Dr. Laura Brayton shares why self-care is mandatory for moms, how chiropractic care improves pregnancy outcomes, and preconception detox essentials.
keyTakeaways: Self-care is not selfish—it's mandatory for mothers because everyone benefits when mom is taking care of herself consistently.
Chiropractic care is safe during all three trimesters of pregnancy and can significantly improve birth outcomes.
Preconception detox should begin six months before conception for both partners, not just the mother.
Your nervous system must be regulated to lead from intuition rather than fear-based decision making.
Life is written in pencil—it's perfectly acceptable to pivot your career priorities when motherhood calls.
Being fully present for one hour a day is more quality time than most children are receiving right now.
High-achieving women can channel their corporate skills into becoming exceptional household CEOs.

pullQuotes.text: Self-care is not selfish—it is mandatory for every mother.
Life is written in pencil—it's okay to pivot careers for motherhood.
Your nervous system must be regulated to lead from intuition, not fear.

timestamps.label: Why chiropractic care is essential during pregnancy
The Webster Technique for smoother natural birth
Nervous system regulation and leadership energy
Masculine vs. feminine energy and motherhood
Life is written in pencil — permission to pivot
Self-care is not selfish — it is mandatory

content:
If you're a high-achieving woman who's been told that self-care is selfish, this episode will completely shift your perspective. Dr. Laura Brayton joins Aimee Rickabus on The Manage Her to deliver a powerful truth: self-care isn't a luxury for mothers—it's mandatory.

As a chiropractor, pediatric specialist, and functional medicine practitioner with over 20 years of experience, Dr. Brayton has witnessed firsthand how holistic approaches to motherhood transform not just individual women, but entire families. Her insights challenge everything you've been told about putting everyone else first.

The Foundation of Holistic Motherhood Wellness

Dr. Brayton's journey into holistic motherhood began with a simple realization: when we address the root causes of health issues rather than just symptoms, everything changes. This philosophy extends far beyond physical health into every aspect of motherhood, from preconception planning to nervous system regulation.

The concept of being "well-adjusted" goes beyond just physical alignment. It encompasses your ability to adapt, respond rather than react, and maintain your center even when life throws curveballs. For high-achieving women, this skill becomes essential as you navigate the complex demands of leadership and motherhood.

Chiropractic Care: Your Secret Weapon During Pregnancy

One of the most eye-opening revelations from this conversation is how chiropractic care can dramatically improve pregnancy outcomes. Dr. Brayton explains that chiropractic adjustments are not only safe during all three trimesters but can actually reduce pregnancy symptoms and create optimal conditions for birth.

The Webster Technique, a specialized chiropractic approach, focuses on aligning the mother's pelvis to give the baby the best possible position for delivery. This isn't just about comfort during pregnancy—it's about creating the conditions for a smoother, potentially shorter labor. When your body is properly aligned, everything functions better, from circulation to nerve communication.

For women who are accustomed to controlling outcomes through sheer willpower and effort, this represents a powerful shift toward working with your body rather than against it. The lesson extends far beyond pregnancy: sometimes the most effective leadership comes from creating optimal conditions rather than forcing results.

Preconception Detox: Planning for Success

Perhaps one of the most actionable insights from Dr. Brayton involves preconception planning. She recommends beginning a detox protocol six months before trying to conceive—and this applies to both partners, not just the mother.

Environmental toxins directly impact fertility and epigenetics, meaning the choices you make before conception influence not just your ability to get pregnant, but the long-term health of your future child. This isn't about perfection; it's about making informed choices that set your family up for success from the very beginning.

For high-achieving women, this kind of strategic planning feels natural. You wouldn't launch a major business initiative without proper preparation, so why approach one of life's most important ventures any differently? The key is applying the same strategic mindset you use in business to your health and family planning.

Nervous System Regulation and Fear-Free Leadership

One of the most profound topics covered in this episode is nervous system regulation and its connection to effective leadership. Dr. Brayton explains that when your nervous system is dysregulated, you make decisions from a place of fear rather than intuition.

This has massive implications for how you show up as both a leader and a mother. When you're constantly in fight-or-flight mode—which many high-achieving women are—you're operating from your survival brain rather than your creative, strategic mind. Learning to regulate your nervous system isn't just about feeling better; it's about making better decisions for yourself and your family.

The conversation touches on how many successful women struggle with the transition from masculine corporate energy to the more receptive energy that motherhood can require. This isn't about abandoning your drive or ambition; it's about expanding your leadership toolkit to include intuition, presence, and the ability to receive support.

Permission to Pivot: Life Is Written in Pencil

Dr. Brayton offers a liberating perspective for women who feel trapped by their previous choices or career trajectories: life is written in pencil. You have permission to pivot, to change priorities, and to redefine success based on your current season of life.

This doesn't mean abandoning your goals or settling for less. Instead, it means recognizing that your corporate skills—strategic thinking, project management, leadership abilities—make you incredibly well-equipped to be what Dr. Brayton calls a "household CEO." The skills that made you successful in business can be channeled into creating an extraordinary family life.

Quality Over Quantity: The One-Hour Rule

For busy mothers who struggle with guilt about not spending enough time with their children, Dr. Brayton offers a refreshing perspective. Being fully present for one hour a day is more quality time than most children are currently receiving.

This insight removes the pressure to be everything to everyone and instead focuses on the power of presence. It's not about the number of hours you spend with your children; it's about the quality of attention you bring to those moments. When you've taken care of yourself and regulated your nervous system, that one hour becomes exponentially more valuable.

The Mandatory Nature of Self-Care

The most powerful message from this episode is Dr. Brayton's assertion that self-care is mandatory, not selfish. When mothers consistently care for themselves, everyone in the family benefits. This isn't about spa days or indulgent treats—though those can be part of it—it's about the fundamental practices that keep you functioning at your best.

This reframe is particularly important for high-achieving women who have been conditioned to believe that putting themselves first is somehow wrong. Dr. Brayton challenges this belief system directly: taking care of yourself is one of the most generous things you can do for your family.

The conversation between Aimee and Dr. Brayton offers a roadmap for women who want to excel in both their careers and their roles as mothers. It's not about choosing one over the other; it's about integrating both in a way that honors your health, your ambitions, and your family's wellbeing. The key is recognizing that sustainable success requires a foundation of wellness that begins with you.
